headline: The Bull Case For Micron Technology (MU) Could Change Following GM Long-Term Auto Memory Supply Deal
summary: In late June 2026, Micron Technology signed a long-term Strategic Customer Agreement with General Motors to supply LPDRAM, NOR and UFS NAND memory and storage for current and next-generation vehicles, underpinned by Micron’s US$2.00 billion Manassas, Virginia DRAM fab modernization that began production earlier this year. This deal adds automotive to Micron’s growing base of multi-year supply contracts, illustrating how AI-era memory shortages are pushing major OEMs to lock in dedicated...

headline: Ford Is Now Down 4 Days in a Row. Is It Time to Switch to General Motors or Stellantis?
summary: Shares of Ford (NYSE:F) are down 2% to $13 in Thursday midday trading, putting the stock on track for a fourth straight down day. The catalyst is a soft Q2 U.S. sales report, and the slide has investors asking whether the money would be better parked in a Detroit rival. Both General Motors (NYSE:GM) and ... headline: With Its First Mass-Market Vehicle Launching, Is Rivian Stock Ready To Turn The Corner?
summary: Rivian (RIVN) is a company at a pivot point. After years of producing its premium R1 electric truck and SUV, it has just begun production of the R2, its first vehicle designed for a mass market. For investors, this is the moment the story changes. The stock has gained 25% over the past year but still trades about 23% below its 52-week high, reflecting both the promise and the peril of this transition. The practical question is whether this operational turning point makes RIVN stock a smart buy r

headline: GM Vs. Ford: GM’s Unified Battery Scale and Aggressive Share Buybacks Make It The Better Buy
summary: General Motors (NYSE: GM) and Ford (NYSE: F) both reported Q1 2026 results in late April, and the earnings reports revealed two very different Detroit strategies. GM leaned on unified Ultium battery scale, a richer sales mix, and heavy share retirement. Ford leaned on hybrids, F-Series demand, and a fast-growing Ford Pro software business while ... GM Vs. Ford: GM’s Unified Battery Scale and Aggressive Share Buybacks Make It The Better Buy

headline: Ford Q2 sales slide 10% on EV drop and model phase-outs; Bronco and Maverick hybrid set records
summary: Ford (F) reported a 10.3% drop in second-quarter US sales, to 549,200 vehicles, as electric-vehicle demand tumbled, F-Series and SUV volumes slipped, and the automaker worked through the discontinuation of two models.

headline: Meta Declares War on the Cloud Giants, and Chip Stocks Pay the Price
summary: Meta's surprise move into cloud computing sent its shares up nearly 9% and rattled the neocloud upstarts, while chip stocks broadly retreated as Wall Street rotated into software. Michael Burry doubled down on his AI short, central bankers debated AI's uncertain economic impact at the ECB's Sintra forum, and oil eased further as US-Iran talks continued in Qatar.

raw.headline: Meta Declares War on the Cloud Giants, and Chip Stocks Pay the Price

Michael Burry doubled down on his AI short, central bankers debated AI's uncertain economic impact at the ECB's Sintra forum, and oil eased further as US-Iran talks continued in Qatar.
